channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: In conservative Utah, some communities are ditching fossil fuel power for clean energy
item.description: In conservative Utah, a coalition of cities and towns shows other communities how to bring new renewable energy to the electric grid in a unique way.
item.content:encoded:
In conservative Utah, a coalition of cities and towns shows other communities how to bring new renewable energy to the electric grid in a unique way.

(Image credit: Kim Raff for NPR)
item.dc:creator: David Condos

item.title: House holds off on prediction market ban despite bipartisan calls for prohibition
item.description: Minority Leader Jeffries is urging Speaker Johnson to "swiftly" hold vote on House prediction market ban.
item.content:encoded:
Minority Leader Jeffries is urging Speaker Johnson to "swiftly" hold vote on House prediction market ban.

(Image credit: Erin Hooley/AP)
item.dc:creator: Luke Garrett

item.title: Steep drop in number of people with Affordable Care Act health coverage, analysis finds
item.description: Enrollment in Healthcare.gov and the other marketplaces is plunging by 5 million, the new paper from KFF finds. Last year, Congress failed to make a deal to keep the coverage more affordable.
item.content:encoded:
Enrollment in Healthcare.gov and the other marketplaces is plunging by 5 million, the new paper from KFF finds. Last year, Congress failed to make a deal to keep the coverage more affordable.

(Image credit: Patrick Sison)
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin

item.title: What we know about how the U.S. government uses spyware (and what we don't)
item.description: Critics of spyware, which can be used to remotely hack into phones, worry the Trump administration is eroding policies that stigmatized the commercial spyware industry.
item.content:encoded:
Critics of spyware, which can be used to remotely hack into phones, worry the Trump administration is eroding policies that stigmatized the commercial spyware industry.

(Image credit: Menahem Kahana)
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block

item.title: 'We're not kids anymore': The DACA generation hits their 30s with an unstable future
item.description: Recipients of the Obama-era DACA program are aging, even as the Trump administration moves to weaken the program's protections and benefits.
item.content:encoded:
Recipients of the Obama-era DACA program are aging, even as the Trump administration moves to weaken the program's protections and benefits.

(Image credit: Ash Ponders for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo

item.title: The French Open courts are clay, a tricky surface for some. Here's how the pros do it
item.description: In tennis, clay has a reputation for being one of the harder surfaces to play on. But a few pros shared some of their tips for staying sharp.
item.content:encoded:
In tennis, clay has a reputation for being one of the harder surfaces to play on. But a few pros shared some of their tips for staying sharp.

(Image credit: Adam Pretty)
item.dc:creator: Ayana Archie

item.title: NTSB hearing will probe cause of fiery UPS jet crash that killed 15 in Louisville
item.description: The nation's top safety investigators will launch a two-day hearing beginning Tuesday into what caused a UPS cargo plane to crash shortly after takeoff in Louisville last year, killing 15 people.
item.content:encoded:
The nation's top safety investigators will launch a two-day hearing beginning Tuesday into what caused a UPS cargo plane to crash shortly after takeoff in Louisville last year, killing 15 people.

(Image credit: Stephen Cohen)
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: These men voted for President Trump. They have very different views of how he's doing
item.description: Two Black men from Georgia who voted for President Trump in 2024 have very different views of how the country is doing now, in the first installment of Swing Shift from NPR's Tamara Keith.
item.content:encoded:
Two Black men from Georgia who voted for President Trump in 2024 have very different views of how the country is doing now, in the first installment of Swing Shift from NPR's Tamara Keith.

(Image credit: Illustrations by Tara Anand)
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith

item.title: Putin visits China to reaffirm Russia ties
item.description: The Kremlin has said Putin and Xi plan to discuss economic cooperation between the two countries, but also "key international and regional issues."
item.content:encoded:
The Kremlin has said Putin and Xi plan to discuss economic cooperation between the two countries, but also "key international and regional issues."

(Image credit: Alexander Kazakov)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Mark Fuhrman, ex-detective convicted of lying during OJ Simpson trial, has died
item.description: Fuhrman was one of the first two police detectives sent to investigate the 1994 killings of OJ Simpson's ex-wife Nicole Brown Simpson and her friend, Ronald Goldman, in Los Angeles.
item.content:encoded:
Fuhrman was one of the first two police detectives sent to investigate the 1994 killings of OJ Simpson's ex-wife Nicole Brown Simpson and her friend, Ronald Goldman, in Los Angeles.

(Image credit: REED SAXON)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
